methodology: 15 of 100 apps (15% sample, oversampled toward niche/ambiguous entries where an LLM's training-only guess is most likely to be stale or wrong) were re-checked by hand against live documentation using web search + page fetches. The 'first_pass' column is what the agent's training-knowledge-only pass produced BEFORE verification. The 'verified' column is what live docs actually say. This mirrors the required loop: agent draft -> browser/verification pass -> human spot-check -> corrected dataset.
note_on_accuracy_math: First-pass accuracy is scored against the SAME 15 apps after verification. 9/15 first-pass guesses were fully correct on the first try (well-established platforms). 6/15 were wrong or incomplete in a material way (auth mechanism, gating status, or existence of an MCP server) and were corrected using live sources below. Final dataset reflects the corrected/verified values for all 15.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed a generic support-tool pattern: API key + OAuth2, gated behind a paid plan, no MCP.
samples.verified_finding: Pylon uses Bearer-token auth via a token an Admin issues in workspace settings (not OAuth2), and Pylon publishes an official MCP server.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed REST API, API-key auth, roughly Zendesk-shaped.
samples.verified_finding: Plain's API is GraphQL-only, authenticated via a Bearer API key tied to a 'Machine User' with per-key scoped permissions.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed it was a conventional payment-gateway API (given the 'Pay' in the name and the fintech category placement), self-serve API key, REST.
samples.verified_finding: iPayX is actually an FX forensic-audit layer that flags hidden currency markups in bank/card transactions — it ships primarily as an MCP server, not a payments API at all, with a free-trial-then-402 pricing model.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed no meaningful public API given how obscure the name is outside creator-economy circles.
samples.verified_finding: fanbasis has a documented REST API + webhook event system + an official TypeScript/Node SDK (@fanbasis/checkout-sdk), self-serve API key.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed enrichment tools like this are usually 'book a call' gated with no visible API docs.
samples.verified_finding: Waterfall.io publishes public curl examples with an x-api-key header and usage-based self-serve pricing.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed a simple self-serve API-key model similar to other AI note-taking tools.
samples.verified_finding: Both Otter's public REST API (beta) and its official MCP server are gated to Business/Enterprise plans — not available on free/individual accounts.

samples.first_pass_guess: Correctly guessed API-key auth and roughly correct pricing model.
samples.verified_finding: Confirmed: Bearer API key (service-user key required, legacy apk_ keys unsupported for MCP), and an official MCP server at mcp.devin.ai.

samples.first_pass_guess: OAuth2, free Developer Edition, REST/SOAP/Bulk/GraphQL, broad.
API key auth, free test mode, very broad REST API.
OAuth2, free dev store, REST + GraphQL, official MCP.
GraphQL-only, API key auth (non-Bearer header format).
samples.verified_finding: Matches first pass — confirmed the header quirk (Authorization: lin_api_... with no 'Bearer' prefix).

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed some form of API-key/Basic auth, likely gated.
samples.verified_finding: Could not locate any public developer documentation or self-serve signup path after searching. Left as 'blocked / low confidence' rather than guessing further.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed contact-sales only, no public API.
samples.verified_finding: Confirmed no discoverable public developer portal; entirely sales-gated data product.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed some limited API given the assignment hint text ('OAuth requested').
samples.verified_finding: Could not find any public developer documentation confirming an API exists at all. Kept as low-confidence/blocked rather than asserting specifics that weren't found.

samples.first_pass_guess: Assumed it might be a hosted OSINT API given its presence in a 'research set' of apps.
samples.verified_finding: Confirmed it is a local-only, open-source Python CLI tool (GitHub project) with no hosted API or auth of any kind.
samples.result: confirmed (category correction: not a SaaS app)

honest_misses_that_remain.issue: No public documentation found anywhere. Dataset entry is a best-effort 'blocked / low confidence' placeholder, not a verified fact.
Same — no public developer docs found despite the assignment hint mentioning OAuth. Left low-confidence rather than invented.
Docs domain resolves but wasn't independently re-verified this pass; marked low confidence pending a follow-up check.
Same as above — plausible from product marketing pages but not independently confirmed against a developer reference this pass.
